informal_statement.text: Given a slice of integers, return a new vector containing the same multiset of elements in nondecreasing order.
preconditions: the input is a slice of i32 values of any length
required_properties: the output length equals the input length
the output is sorted in nondecreasing order
the output is a permutation of the input (same multiset of elements)
edge_cases: slice of length that is a power of two
slice of length that is not a power of two

semantic_units.description: Length preservation: the output has the same length as the input.
common_failure_modes: stating only a length bound instead of equality

semantic_units.description: Sortedness: the output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
Permutation: the output is a permutation of the input (equal multiset of elements).
Stability is NOT required by the spec.

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output length equals the input length.
The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
The output is a permutation of the input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mergeSort_length (xs : Arr) : (mergeSort xs).length = xs.length :=
  CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.MergeSortTheory.mergeSort_length xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output list has the same length as the input list.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mergeSort_sorted (xs : Arr) : Sorted (mergeSort xs) := by
  simpa [Sorted, mergeSort] using
    CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.MergeSortTheory.mergeSort_sorted xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output list is sorted in nondecreasing order.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mergeSort_perm (xs : Arr) : IsPerm (mergeSort xs) xs := by
  simpa [IsPerm, IsPermutation, mergeSort] using
    CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.MergeSortTheory.mergeSort_perm xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output list is a permutation of the input list (same multiset of elements).
